name: Soybeans
summary: The one common plant food that's a complete protein — and whose soy protein has long-standing evidence for modestly lowering LDL cholesterol.
superfoodReason: A rare complete plant protein (all essential amino acids) with a decades-old evidence base and an FDA health claim for lowering cholesterol.

benefits.mechanism: Unlike most plant foods, soybeans supply all nine essential amino acids in usable amounts, making them a full protein source for building and maintaining muscle without the saturated fat of meat.

citations.title: Protein — The Nutrition Source, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health

benefits.claim: Soy protein can lower LDL cholesterol
benefits.mechanism: Replacing animal protein with soy protein modestly lowers LDL cholesterol — the effect behind the FDA's soy-and-heart-disease health claim. A landmark meta-analysis quantified the reduction in serum cholesterol from soy protein intake; later reviews find a smaller but real effect.

citations.title: Meta-analysis of the effects of soy protein intake on serum lipids (Anderson et al., N Engl J Med 1995)

surprisingFacts.text: Edamame are just immature green soybeans, harvested before they harden — which is why they're eaten fresh from the pod rather than dried.
